prompt:
For our team's Friday bot league I want a rock-paper-scissors tournament engine in C# — bots are deterministic strategies, matches are round-robin, and the whole run prints a transcript the league channel can diff week to week. Namespace RpsBots; the xunit suite in RpsBotsTests.cs is the contract (dotnet test); roster fixture in testdata/roster.txt; nothing beyond what the csproj pulls.

Core types:
- enum Move { Rock, Paper, Scissors }, rendered ROCK/PAPER/SCISSORS in transcripts.
- static class Rules: Beats(a, b) — rock beats scissors, scissors beats paper, paper beats rock; Counter(m) — the move that beats m.
- interface IBot { string Name { get; } Move Next(IReadOnlyList<Move> opponentMoves); } — a bot only ever sees the OPPONENT's history, in round order.

The four strategies:
- ConstantBot(name, move) — always the same move.
- CyclerBot(name, sequence) — round k (0-based, k = opponent moves seen so far) plays sequence[k % length]. Empty sequence is an ArgumentException.
- CopyLastBot(name) — first round Rock, afterwards the opponent's previous move.
- FrequencyBot(name) — tallies the opponent's moves so far and plays the counter of the most frequent one. Ties (including the no-history all-zero tally) resolve to whichever tied move comes first in the order Rock, Paper, Scissors — so its opening move is always Paper.

Match: static Match.Play(a, b, rounds) -> MatchResult with WinsA/WinsB/Draws and a Transcript, one line per round: 'R{n}: {A} {MOVE} vs {B} {MOVE} -> {winnerName}' ('-> draw' when tied), n starting at 1. rounds < 1 -> ArgumentOutOfRangeException.

Tournament: static Tournament.Run(rosterText, roundsPerMatch) -> one string. Roster file: one bot per line — '{Name} constant {R|P|S}', '{Name} cycler {sequence like RPS}', '{Name} copylast', '{Name} counter' (that last one is the frequency bot); '#' comments and blank lines ignored. Unknown kind or bad move letters -> FormatException; duplicate names -> ArgumentException; fewer than two bots -> ArgumentException. Every pair plays once, pairs in roster order (first vs each later, and so on). Output: for each match a 'MATCH {A} vs {B}' line, the round lines, then 'RESULT {A} {winsA} {B} {winsB}'; after all matches a 'STANDINGS' line and one line per bot: '{pos}. {name} pts={pts} w={w} d={d} l={l} rw={roundWins} rl={roundLosses}'. Match points: win 3, draw 1, loss 0 (a match is drawn when round wins are equal). Standings order is a pinned cascade: points desc, then round-win difference (rw-rl) desc, then rw desc, then name ascending (ordinal). Positions are just 1..n down the sorted table.

The suite diffs an entire fixture league run character for character, so the strategies, pairing order and the tie-break cascade all have to be exact. Don't touch RpsBotsTests.cs, RpsBots.csproj, testdata/ or .gitignore.
notes: 7G9 item 6. RPS tournament: four pinned deterministic strategies, round-robin engine, per-round transcripts, standings with a genuinely exercised tie-break cascade (fixture league: Rocky and Echo tie on points AND rw-rl, decided by rw; name tie-break covered by a two-constant-bot roster). Full fixture-league output generated and cross-checked with an independent Python simulation; sample rounds hand-verified.
